SEARCH

Class CharacterSearchManager

java.lang.Object
CharacterSearchManager
public class CharacterSearchManager extends Object
Manages character searches based on hit points and speed values. Automatically determines and displays valid ranges.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Christopher Perez

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description

    allCharacters

    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    displayLargestSpeedGroup()
    Displays the largest speed group (the speed value with the most characters) If multiple speed groups have the same maximum size, all are displayed
    void
    displayResults(TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> characters)
    Displays a TreeSet of Pokemon characters
    void
    displaySpeedStatistics()
    Displays statistics about speed distribution Shows number of unique speed values and distribution
    void
    displayTop3SpeedGroups()
    Displays the top 3 speed groups and lists characters in each group A speed group consists of all characters sharing the same speed value
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findHighestHitPoint()
    Finds character(s) with the highest hit point value
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findHighestSpeed()
    Finds character(s) with the highest speed value
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findLowestHitPoint()
    Finds character(s) with the lowest hit point value
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findLowestSpeed()
    Finds character(s) with the lowest speed value
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findTop3FastestSpeeds()
    Finds characters with the top 3 fastest speeds
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    findTop3SlowestSpeeds()
    Finds characters with the top 3 slowest speeds
    int[]
    getHitPointRange()
    Gets the minimum and maximum hit points from all characters
    Map<Integer,Integer>
    getSpeedDistribution()
    Gets all unique speed values and their counts Useful for debugging and analysis
    int[]
    getSpeedRange()
    Gets the minimum and maximum speed values from all characters
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    searchByHitPointRange()
    Searches for characters within a range of hit points Returns results sorted by hit points in ascending order
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    searchBySpecificHitPoint()
    Searches for characters with a specific hit point value Returns results sorted by hit points in ascending order
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    searchBySpecificSpeed()
    Searches for characters with a specific speed value Returns results sorted by speed in ascending order
    TreeSet<PokemonCharacter>
    searchBySpeedRange()
    Searches for characters within a range of speed values Returns results sorted by speed in ascending order

  • Method Details

    • getHitPointRange

      public int[] getHitPointRange()
      Gets the minimum and maximum hit points from all characters
      Returns:
      int array [min, max]

    • getSpeedRange

      public int[] getSpeedRange()
      Gets the minimum and maximum speed values from all characters
      Returns:
      int array [min, max]

    • searchBySpecificHitPoint

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> searchBySpecificHitPoint()
      Searches for characters with a specific hit point value Returns results sorted by hit points in ascending order
      Returns:
      TreeSet of matching characters

    • searchByHitPointRange

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> searchByHitPointRange()
      Searches for characters within a range of hit points Returns results sorted by hit points in ascending order
      Returns:
      TreeSet of matching characters

    • findLowestHitPoint

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findLowestHitPoint()
      Finds character(s) with the lowest hit point value
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters with lowest HP

    • findHighestHitPoint

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findHighestHitPoint()
      Finds character(s) with the highest hit point value
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters with highest HP

    • searchBySpecificSpeed

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> searchBySpecificSpeed()
      Searches for characters with a specific speed value Returns results sorted by speed in ascending order
      Returns:
      TreeSet of matching characters

    • searchBySpeedRange

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> searchBySpeedRange()
      Searches for characters within a range of speed values Returns results sorted by speed in ascending order
      Returns:
      TreeSet of matching characters

    • findLowestSpeed

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findLowestSpeed()
      Finds character(s) with the lowest speed value
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters with lowest speed

    • findHighestSpeed

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findHighestSpeed()
      Finds character(s) with the highest speed value
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters with highest speed

    • findTop3FastestSpeeds

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findTop3FastestSpeeds()
      Finds characters with the top 3 fastest speeds
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters sorted by speed in descending order

    • findTop3SlowestSpeeds

      public TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> findTop3SlowestSpeeds()
      Finds characters with the top 3 slowest speeds
      Returns:
      TreeSet of characters sorted by speed in ascending order

    • displayTop3SpeedGroups

      public void displayTop3SpeedGroups()
      Displays the top 3 speed groups and lists characters in each group A speed group consists of all characters sharing the same speed value

    • displayLargestSpeedGroup

      public void displayLargestSpeedGroup()
      Displays the largest speed group (the speed value with the most characters) If multiple speed groups have the same maximum size, all are displayed

    • getSpeedDistribution

      public Map<Integer,Integer> getSpeedDistribution()
      Gets all unique speed values and their counts Useful for debugging and analysis
      Returns:
      Map of speed values to count of characters with that speed

    • displaySpeedStatistics

      public void displaySpeedStatistics()
      Displays statistics about speed distribution Shows number of unique speed values and distribution

    • displayResults

      public void displayResults(TreeSet<PokemonCharacter> characters)
      Displays a TreeSet of Pokemon characters
      Parameters:
      characters - The TreeSet to display
